response.justification: The candidate is a strong generalist with nearly two decades of software experience, but their profile does not match this role's core requirements. Their C++ experience is limited to a short seven-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015 and a university thesis project, far from the 5-10 years of recent, hands-on Windows C++ development required. Their database work centers on MongoDB and MySQL rather than SQL Server or Oracle, and they have no fixed-income or capital-markets domain exposure beyond a brief fintech role (RIDE Capital) that used Node.js/TypeScript, not C++. The gap between their web/full-stack background and this specialized capital-markets C++ role is too wide to justify an interview.
strengths: Long, varied engineering career with strong craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, pairing)
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Some fintech exposure at RIDE Capital (investment platform in Germany)
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and roughly a decade old, with no Windows-platform C++ depth
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; database background is MongoDB/MySQL
No fixed-income, derivatives, or capital-markets domain knowledge

key_factors.factor: Solid general engineering fundamentals and testing culture
